Verify fresh snapshot creation\n40\tkubectl logs deployment/queue-api -c litestream -n devimprint --tail=100 | grep -i \"snapshot\\|generation\"\n41\t```\n42\t\n43\t## Detailed Steps\n44\t\n45\t### Step 1: Verify Pre-Execution State\n46\t\n47\t```bash\n48\t# Check ARMOR version\n49\tkubectl get deployment armor -n devimprint -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image}'\n50\t# Expected: ronaldraygun/armor:0.1.467 or higher\n51\t\n52\t# Check queue-api status\n53\tkubectl get pods -n devimprint -l app=queue-api\n54\t# Expected: 1 running pod with 2/2 containers ready\n55\t\n56\t# Check litestream is running\n57\tkubectl logs deployment/queue-api -c litestream -n devimprint --tail=20\n58\t# Should show replication activity\n59\t```\n60\t\n61\t### Step 2: Scale Down queue-api\n62\t\n63\t**IMPORTANT**: This stops the queue-api service briefly (~1-2 minutes).\n64\t\n65\t```bash\n66\t# Scale down to zero replicas\n67\tkubectl scale deployment queue-api --replicas=0 -n devimprint\n68\t\n69\t# Wait for pod termination\n70\tkubectl wait --for=delete pod -l app=queue-api -n devimprint --timeout=120s\n71\t\n72\t# Verify no pods running\n73\tkubectl get pods -n devimprint -l app=queue-api\n74\t# Expected: No resources found\n75\t```\n76\t\n77\t**Impact**: Queue-api will be unavailable during this period. Execution Guide: Force Fresh Litestream Backup Baseline (bf-36zo2)\n\n## Overview\n\nThis guide provides step-by-step instructions to force queue-api's litestream to establish a fresh backup baseline after ARMOR has been upgraded to the fixed version (0.1.467).\n\n## Prerequisites\n\n- ARMOR is running version 0.1.467 or higher (✅ VERIFIED: currently 0.1.467)\n- You have kubectl access to ord-devimprint cluster with write permissions\n- Queue-api is currently running and healthy\n\n## Quick Reference\n\n```bash\n# 1. Check current state\nkubectl get deployment queue-api -n devimprint\nkubectl get pods -n devimprint -l app=queue-api\n\n# 2. Scale down queue-api\nkubectl scale deployment queue-api --replicas=0 -n devimprint\nkubectl wait --for=delete pod -l app=queue-api -n devimprint --timeout=120s\n\n# 3. Apply and run the job\nkubectl apply -f /home/coding/ARMOR/notes/litestream-force-fresh-snapshot-job.yaml\n\n# 4. Monitor job execution\nkubectl logs job/litestream-force-fresh-snapshot -n devimprint -f\nkubectl wait --for=condition=complete job/litestream-force-fresh-snapshot -n devimprint --timeout=300s\n\n# 5. Check job results\nkubectl logs job/litestream-force-fresh-snapshot -n devimprint\nkubectl delete job litestream-force-fresh-snapshot -n devimprint\n\n# 6. Scale up queue-api\nkubectl scale deployment queue-api --replicas=1 -n devimprint\nkubectl wait --for=condition=available deployment/queue-api -n devimprint --timeout=180s\n\n# 7. Verify fresh snapshot creation\nkubectl logs deployment/queue-api -c litestream -n devimprint --tail=100 | grep -i \"snapshot\\|generation\"\n```\n\n## Detailed Steps\n\n### Step 1: Verify Pre-Execution State\n\n```bash\n# Check ARMOR version\nkubectl get deployment armor -n devimprint -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image}'\n# Expected: ronaldraygun/armor:0.1.467 or higher\n\n# Check queue-api status\nkubectl get pods -n devimprint -l app=queue-api\n# Expected: 1 running pod with 2/2 containers ready\n\n# Check litestream is running\nkubectl logs deployment/queue-api -c litestream -n devimprint --tail=20\n# Should show replication activity\n```\n\n### Step 2: Scale Down queue-api\n\n**IMPORTANT**: This stops the queue-api service briefly (~1-2 minutes).\n\n```bash\n# Scale down to zero replicas\nkubectl scale deployment queue-api --replicas=0 -n devimprint\n\n# Wait for pod termination\nkubectl wait --for=delete pod -l app=queue-api -n devimprint --timeout=120s\n\n# Verify no pods running\nkubectl get pods -n devimprint -l app=queue-api\n# Expected: No resources found\n```\n\n**Impact**: Queue-api will be unavailable during this period. The seeder/workers will retry and queue naturally repopulates on restart.\n\n### Step 3: Apply the Fresh Snapshot Job\n\n```bash\n# Apply the job YAML\nkubectl apply -f /home/coding/ARMOR/notes/litestream-force-fresh-snapshot-job.yaml\n\n# Verify job was created\nkubectl get job litestream-force-fresh-snapshot -n devimprint\n```\n\n### Step 4: Monitor Job Execution\n\n```bash\n# Watch job logs in real-time\nkubectl logs job/litestream-force-fresh-snapshot -n devimprint -f\n\n# Or wait for completion (background)\nkubectl wait --for=condition=complete job/litestream-force-fresh-snapshot -n devimprint --timeout=300s\n\n# Check final job status\nkubectl describe job litestream-force-fresh-snapshot -n devimprint\n```\n\n**Expected Job Output**:\n```\n=== Litestream Fresh Snapshot Job ===\nTask: bf-36zo2\nTimestamp: [timestamp]\n\nStep 1: Checking database exists\n✓ Database found: /data/queue.db\n\nStep 2: Displaying litestream configuration\n[config content]\n\nStep 3: Checking current litestream status\n[database status or \"no databases found\"]\n\nStep 4: Clearing litestream local state\nThis forces a fresh snapshot on next sync\n[reset output]\n\nStep 5: Verifying reset\n✓ Litestream state successfully cleared\n\n=== Job Complete ===\nNext steps:\n1. Scale queue-api deployment back to 1\n2. Monitor litestream logs for new snapshot creation\n3. Verify new generation in ARMOR S3 bucket\n```\n\n### Step 5: Cleanup Job\n\n```bash\n# Get final job logs\nkubectl logs job/litestream-force-fresh-snapshot -n devimprint > /tmp/litestream-reset.log\n\n# Delete the job\nkubectl delete job litestream-force-fresh-snapshot -n devimprint\n```\n\n### Step 6: Scale Up queue-api\n\n```bash\n# Scale back to 1 replica\nkubectl scale deployment queue-api --replicas=1 -n devimprint\n\n# Wait for pod to be ready\nkubectl wait --for=condition=available deployment/queue-api -n devimprint --timeout=180s\n\n# Verify pod is healthy\nkubectl get pods -n devimprint -l app=queue-api\n# Expected: 1 running pod with 2/2 containers ready\n```\n\n### Step 7: Verify Fresh Snapshot Creation\n\n**This is the critical verification step.**\n\n```bash\n# Monitor litestream logs for snapshot creation\nkubectl logs deployment/queue-api -c litestream -n devimprint -f --tail=100 | grep -i \"snapshot\\|generation\"\n\n# Look for messages like:\n# - \"taking new snapshot\"\n# - \"generating new snapshot\"\n# - \"snapshot uploaded\"\n# - generation IDs\n```\n\n**Expected Behavior**:\n1. Litestream sidecar starts\n2. Detects no local state (cleared by reset job)\n3. Initiates fresh snapshot upload to ARMOR\n4. Logs show snapshot creation progress\n5. Continues normal WAL replication\n\n### Step 8: Note Generation ID\n\n```bash\n# Extract generation ID from litestream logs\nkubectl logs deployment/queue-api -c litestream -n devimprint --tail=200 | grep -i \"generation\" > /tmp/litestream-generation.log\n\n# Or check litestream databases status (if you can exec)\nkubectl exec -n devimprint deployment/queue-api -c litestream -- litestream databases\n```\n\n**Save the generation ID** for the next verification step (restore testing).\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### Job Fails to Start\n\n```bash\n# Check job status\nkubectl describe job litestream-force-fresh-snapshot -n devimprint\n\n# Common issues:\n# - PVC not mounted (check volume claim name)\n# - ConfigMap not found (check litestream-config)\n# - Secrets not accessible (check armor-writer secret)\n```\n\n### Job Times Out\n\n```bash\n# Check job pod logs\nkubectl logs -l app=litestream-reset -n devimprint\n\n# Increase timeout if needed\n# Edit activeDeadlineSeconds in job YAML\n```\n\n### No Fresh Snapshot Created\n\n```bash\n# Verify litestream configuration\nkubectl exec -n devimprint deployment/queue-api -c litestream -- cat /etc/litestream.yml\n\n# Check litestream can reach ARMOR\nkubectl exec -n devimprint deployment/queue-api -c litestream -- litestream replicate -v\n\n# Manually trigger snapshot (if needed)\nkubectl exec -n devimprint deployment/queue-api -c litestream -- litestream snapshot /data/queue.db\n```\n\n### Queue-api Unhealthy After Scale-Up\n\n```bash\n# Check pod status\nkubectl get pods -n devimprint -l app=queue-api\n\n# Check logs\nkubectl logs deployment/queue-api -c queue-api -n devimprint --tail=100\n\n# Common issues:\n# - Database locked (litestream still initializing)\n# - PVC mount issues\n# - Resource constraints\n```\n\n## Success Criteria\n\nYou have successfully completed this task when:\n\n- [x] Confirmed litestream is configured for queue-api database\n- [x] Created Kubernetes Job to trigger fresh snapshot/generation\n- [ ] Verified new backup generation is active and replicating\n- [ ] Noted the generation ID for the next verification step\n\n## Next Steps (Separate Tasks)\n\n1. **bf-4qq1**: Complete ARMOR upgrade verification (parent task)\n2. **Restore Testing**: Test restore from the fresh snapshot to verify backup integrity\n3. **Cleanup**: Remove old backup generations (after verification succeeds)\n4. **Documentation**: Update runbooks with new backup generation ID\n\n## Files Created\n\n- `/home/coding/ARMOR/notes/bf-36zo2-litestream-fresh-snapshot.md` - messages**:\n68\t- `taking new snapshot`\n69\t- `generating new snapshot`\n70\t- `generation` followed by a new generation ID (e.g., `0000000000000002` or higher)\n71\t\n72\t### Step 9: Note the generation ID\n73\tRecord the generation ID from the logs for the next verification step (bf-5uehq). This is needed to verify the backup is valid and can be restored.\n74\t\n75\t### Step 10: Cleanup (optional)\n76\t```bash\n77\tkubectl --server=http://kubectl-proxy-ord-devimprint:8001 delete job litestream-force-fresh-snapshot -n devimprint\n78\t```\n79\t\n80\t## Verification Checklist\n81\t\n82\t- [ ] Job completed successfully (exit code 0)\n83\t- [ ] queue-api scaled back up and healthy\n84\t- [ ] Litestream logs show \"taking new snapshot\" or similar\n85\t- [ ] New generation ID noted for downstream verification\n86\t- [ ] Job cleanup completed (optional)\n87\t\n88\t## What the Reset Does\n89\t\n90\tThe `litestream reset /data/queue.db` command:\n91\t1. New snapshot is created from the fixed ARMOR version\n95\t\n96\tThis ensures the backup chain starts from a clean baseline, free of any multipart corruption that may have occurred with the old ARMOR version.\n97\t\n98\t## Next Steps\n99\t\n100\tAfter completion:\n101\t1. This is critical because existing backups may contain the multipart corruption bug.\n\n## Prerequisites Verified ✅\n\n1. **Litestream Configuration**: Confirmed for queue-api database\n   - Database path: `/data/queue.db`\n   - PVC: `queue-api-data-sata-2`\n   - ConfigMap: `queue-api-litestream-config`\n   - Replication target: ARMOR S3 (`http://armor:9000`) → `devimprint/state/litestream/queue.db`\n\n2. **Current State**: queue-api deployment running 1 replica\n   - Litestream sidecar: active and replicating\n   - Init container: `litestream-restore` (handles recovery on startup)\n\n## Execution Steps\n\nThese steps require write access to ord-devimprint cluster. Execute in order:\n\n### Step 1: Scale down queue-api\n```bash\nkubectl --server=http://kubectl-proxy-ord-devimprint:8001 scale deployment queue-api --replicas=0 -n devimprint\n```\n\n### Step 2: Wait for pod termination\n```bash\nkubectl --server=http://kubectl-proxy-ord-devimprint:8001 wait --for=delete pod -l app=queue-api -n devimprint --timeout=60s\n```\n\n### Step 3: Apply the reset job\n```bash\nkubectl --server=http://kubectl-proxy-ord-devimprint:8001 apply -f /home/coding/ARMOR/notes/litestream-force-fresh-snapshot-job.yaml\n```\n\n### Step 4: Monitor job completion\n```bash\nkubectl --server=http://kubectl-proxy-ord-devimprint:8001 wait --for=condition=complete job/litestream-force-fresh-snapshot -n devimprint --timeout=300s\n```\n\n### Step 5: Check job logs\n```bash\nkubectl --server=http://kubectl-proxy-ord-devimprint:8001 logs job/litestream-force-fresh-snapshot -n devimprint\n```\n\nExpected output includes:\n- `✓ Database found: /data/queue.db`\n- `Configuration: [...]` (showing litestream config)\n- `✓ Litestream state successfully cleared` or similar\n\n### Step 6: Scale up queue-api\n```bash\nkubectl --server=http://kubectl-proxy-ord-devimprint:8001 scale deployment queue-api --replicas=1 -n devimprint\n```\n\n### Step 7: Wait for pod ready\n```bash\nkubectl --server=http://kubectl-proxy-ord-devimprint:8001 wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=queue-api -n devimprint --timeout=120s\n```\n\n### Step 8: Verify fresh snapshot creation\n```bash\nkubectl --server=http://kubectl-proxy-ord-devimprint:8001 logs deployment/queue-api -c litestream -n devimprint --tail=50\n```\n\n**Look for these log messages**:\n- `taking new snapshot`\n- `generating new snapshot`\n- `generation` followed by a new generation ID (e.g., `0000000000000002` or higher)\n\n### Step 9: Note the generation ID\nRecord the generation ID from the logs for the next verification step (bf-5uehq). This is needed to verify the backup is valid and can be restored.\n\n### Step 10: Cleanup (optional)\n```bash\nkubectl --server=http://kubectl-proxy-ord-devimprint:8001 delete job litestream-force-fresh-snapshot -n devimprint\n```\n\n## Verification Checklist\n\n- [ ] Job completed successfully (exit code 0)\n- [ ] queue-api scaled back up and healthy\n- [ ] Litestream logs show \"taking new snapshot\" or similar\n- [ ] New generation ID noted for downstream verification\n- [ ] Job cleanup completed (optional)\n\n## What the Reset Does\n\nThe `litestream reset /data/queue.db` command:\n1. Clears litestream's local tracking database for `/data/queue.db`\n2. On next startup, litestream sees no prior state\n3. Triggers creation of a new snapshot generation\n4. New snapshot is created from the fixed ARMOR version\n\nThis ensures the backup chain starts from a clean baseline, free of any multipart corruption that may have occurred with the old ARMOR version.\n\n## Next Steps\n\nAfter completion:\n1. Use the noted generation ID for backup restore verification (see bf-5uehq)\n2. Monitor litestream logs for ongoing replication\n3. Verify no corruption errors in ARMOR logs for the new objects\n\n## References\n\n- Job file: `/home/coding/ARMOR/notes/litestream-force-fresh-snapshot-job.yaml`\n- ADR on multipart corruption: `/home/coding/ARMOR/docs/adr/002-multipart-corruption-detection-gaps.md`\n- Bead: bf-36zo2\n","numLines":110,"startLine":1,"totalLines":110}}}

Establish a clean baseline from the fixed ARMOR version\n2. Ensure backup chain is free of multipart corruption\n3.
